context.project_description: Implement a token usage tracking system that instruments LLM provider methods to count input/output tokens for chat, embedding, and vision operations without modifying existing provider interfaces.
key_requirements: Instrument ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, and VisionProvider methods to track token usage
Count tokens for messages input and responses output in chat operations
Track token counts for text inputs in embedding operations
Capture image processing token usage in vision operations
constraints: Cannot modify existing provider interface protocols in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Must integrate with existing InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook infrastructure
Token counting must work across different LLM providers without hardcoding model-specific logic
Implementation should not break current API contracts
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py contains BenchmarkHook protocol and InstrumentedProxy class that can be used for method interception; fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py defines the provider protocols including ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider, and VisionProvider; fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, anthropic.py, cohere.py, ollama.py contain concrete implementations of these providers
stakeholders: user requesting token usage tracking to see costs per query

in_scope: instrument chat methods to count input/output tokens
instrument embedding methods to count input/output tokens
instrument vision methods to count input/output tokens
implement hook-based instrumentation system using existing infrastructure
track token usage per query with context
out_of_scope: modifying base provider interfaces or protocols
adding explicit cost calculation based on pricing tiers
tracking aggregated token usage over time
handling streaming chat responses for token counting

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_counter.py — Implements TokenCounterBenchmark hook to track LLM token usage for chat, embed, and vision methods
fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/__init__.py — Exports the token counter functionality

assumptions.assumption: The existing provider interfaces can be instrumented without modification to avoid breaking changes
assumptions.impact: If we cannot properly wrap the methods, token tracking will fail or be incomplete

assumptions.assumption: The tiktoken library will provide accurate token counting for all supported LLMs
assumptions.impact: Inaccurate token counts would lead to incorrect cost calculations and usage tracking

assumptions.assumption: Model information (name, tier) is not exposed through current provider interfaces but can be accessed via configuration or context
assumptions.impact: Without model details, we cannot provide accurate pricing insights, only raw token usage

assumptions.assumption: The InstrumentedProxy and BenchmarkHook infrastructure in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py is sufficient for implementing the tracking system
assumptions.impact: If this infrastructure is inadequate, we'll need to build custom instrumentation logic which increases complexity and risk

assumptions.assumption: Per-query token tracking is sufficient for the use case based on 'how many LLM tokens each query costs'
assumptions.impact: If aggregated metrics are needed instead, we'll need to modify the approach significantly to accumulate data over time

assumptions.assumption: The current provider implementations return strings or lists of floats as defined in base interfaces
assumptions.impact: If streaming responses or other return types exist that aren't accounted for, token counting logic may break

approaches.name: InstrumentedProxy Hook Approach
approaches.description: Leverage existing InstrumentedProxy system with custom BenchmarkHook to track token usage via request/response payload sizes
pros: Uses existing infrastructure without modifying base protocols
cons: Requires careful hook implementation to accurately count tokens
May need additional logic to distinguish between input/output tokens
best_for: Production systems with existing instrumentation infrastructure

approaches.name: Answer Extension Approach
approaches.description: Extend Answer dataclass to include token usage fields and modify wrapping logic to populate them
pros: Clear separation of concerns for token tracking data
Easy to access token metrics from answers
cons: Requires updating answer creation logic throughout the system
May need changes in serialization/deserialization paths
best_for: Systems where token usage data is frequently accessed from Answer objects
Projects prioritizing explicit data modeling over infrastructure reuse

architecture.reasoning: The InstrumentedProxy Hook Approach is correct because it directly leverages the existing instrumentation system already present in core/instrumentation.py which provides the exact mechanism needed for token tracking through on_call_start/on_call_end hooks. The other approach of extending Answer is wrong because: 1) It unnecessarily modifies an existing data structure when the infrastructure already exists to capture metrics, and 2) It creates tight coupling between token counting logic and answer representation rather than using the established hook pattern.

key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_vs_modification: Using existing InstrumentedProxy system avoids modifying core interfaces and LLM client structure, but requires implementing custom hook logic for token counting
key_tradeoffs.minimal_extension_vs_full_rearchitecture: Extending Answer class with token fields is a minimal change compared to redefining entire provider interfaces

technology_considerations: InstrumentedProxy system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py for hook-based instrumentation
BenchmarkHook implementation for capturing request/response sizes during LLM calls
Dataclass extension approach for Answer class to add token usage fields without breaking existing contracts
Direct provider instantiation via factory functions instead of LLM client modification
architecture.scope_statement: Implement a custom BenchmarkHook for tracking LLM request/response payload sizes to estimate token usage, wrap LLM providers with InstrumentedProxy, and optionally extend Answer class minimally to store token counts when needed.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking via Instrumentation System
adrs.context: The need to track token usage for LLM calls without modifying existing provider interfaces or core architecture components.
adrs.decision: Leverage the existing InstrumentedProxy system and BenchmarkHook protocol to instrument LLM providers and track request/response sizes as a proxy for token counts.
adrs.rationale: This approach avoids modifying existing base protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider) that are already well-defined and stable. It uses the extant instrumentation infrastructure which is designed specifically for this kind of observability. The solution maintains backward compatibility while enabling token tracking through hook-based metrics collection.
consequences: Minimal code changes required - only new BenchmarkHook implementation and wrapping logic
No breaking changes to existing LLM provider interfaces or Answer class structure
Token tracking becomes opt-in via hook registration rather than mandatory architectural change
Maintains separation of concerns with instrumentation being a cross-cutting concern
alternatives_considered: Alternative 1 — Modify base provider protocols to add token tracking parameters – rejected because it would break existing implementations and require extensive refactoring of all provider classes.
Alternative 2 — Create new token-aware interfaces – rejected because current protocols are sufficient; the concern can be addressed via composition through InstrumentedProxy.

Alternative 3 — Add explicit token fields to Answer class – rejected because this introduces unnecessary complexity when metrics can be collected externally and only needed for display or analytics purposes.

adrs.title: ADR: InstrumentedProxy Hook Approach vs Direct Answer Extension
adrs.context: Choosing between instrumenting through existing hooks in core/instrumentation.py versus extending the Answer data structure for token tracking
adrs.decision: Use InstrumentedProxy Hook Approach with on_call_start/on_call_end hooks
adrs.rationale: The hook approach directly leverages existing infrastructure and provides clean separation of concerns. The alternative of modifying Answer creates tight coupling between metrics collection and core data structures, making the system less flexible for future instrumentation needs.
consequences: Token counting logic becomes decoupled from answer structure - this reduces cohesion but increases modularity
Instrumentation infrastructure must be robust enough to handle all call types (OpenAIChat, OpenAIEmbedding, AnthropicChat) without modification
The v1 implementation excludes direct integration with Answer data structures that might provide more detailed token breakdowns
alternatives_considered: Extending Answer class with token_count fields - this would tightly couple metrics collection to core data models and make the system less extensible
Creating a separate token tracking service - this would add complexity without leveraging existing infrastructure that already handles hooks appropriately

components.name: FitzKragEngine
responsibilities: Load and initialize components for query processing
Route queries through appropriate retrieval strategies
Generate answers using LLMs and contextual information
interfaces: ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional[ConversationContext]) -> Answer

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wraps objects to instrument method calls for benchmarking and caching
responsibilities: Track performance metrics of LLM provider methods
Enable hook-based instrumentation for observability
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: OpenAIChat
components.purpose: Wrapper for OpenAI chat completion API
responsibilities: Handle chat requests using OpenAI's models
Stream responses from the LLM
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, base_url: str | None, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

components.name: OpenAIEmbedding
responsibilities: Generate embeddings for text using OpenAI models
Support batch operations and dimension specification
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, dimensions: int | None, base_url: str | None) -> None

components.name: AnthropicChat
responsibilities: Handle chat requests using Anthropic's models
Stream responses from the LLM
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

components.name: CohereChat
components.purpose: Wrapper for Cohere API chat completion
responsibilities: Handle chat requests using Cohere models
Stream responses from the LLM
interfaces: __init__(auth: AuthProvider, model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

components.name: OllamaChat
components.purpose: Wrapper for Ollama local LLM API
responsibilities: Handle chat requests using locally running models via HTTP
Stream responses from the LLM
interfaces: __init__(model: str | None, tier: ModelTier, base_url: str | None, models: dict[ModelTier, str] | None) -> None

components.name: BenchmarkHook
components.purpose: Protocol for instrumenting method calls with performance tracking
responsibilities: Capture start and end events of LLM provider calls
Record metrics like request/response sizes
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

integration_points: ExternalSystem — InstrumentedProxy system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py can be used to wrap LLM providers for token counting
ExternalSystem — BenchmarkHook protocol in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py can be implemented to capture request/response sizes for token tracking
ExternalSystem — Existing provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider) in fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py don't need modification and can be instrumented directly

artifacts.purpose: Provides the instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance, including hook registration and proxy wrapping mechanisms used for token counting.
Defines the Answer dataclass that represents structured responses from the knowledge engine, which may need minimal extension for token tracking.
Defines the base protocol interfaces for LLM providers including ChatProvider which returns strings rather than structured objects.

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
deliverables: Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py
Verified: fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py
phases.verification_command: pytest fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_base.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_openai.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_cohere.py fitz_ai/llm/providers/test_ollama.py

phases.name: Identify Relevant Providers
phases.objective: Determine which LLM providers support token counting in their chat responses
deliverables: List of supported providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Ollama) that return token usage data
key_risks: Providers may not support token counting or have inconsistent response formats

phases.name: Define Token Usage Interface
phases.objective: Create a standardized data structure for token usage information
deliverables: TokenUsage dataclass in fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py with prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, total_tokens fields
key_risks: Data structure may not be compatible with existing response handling patterns

phases.name: Augment Provider Responses
phases.objective: Modify chat methods in supported providers to include token usage information in responses
deliverables: Updated OpenAIChat.chat() with token parsing and wrapping
Updated AnthropicChat.chat() with token parsing and wrapping
Updated CohereChat.chat() with token parsing and wrapping
Updated OllamaChat.chat() with token parsing and wrapping
key_risks: Different providers may have different response structures for token usage data

phases.name: Integrate with Logging System
phases.objective: Add logging of token usage metrics to existing logging infrastructure
deliverables: Modified FitzKragEngine.answer() method that logs prompt/completion tokens using structured logging
Enhanced fitz.py SDK to log token usage per query
key_risks: Logging may interfere with performance or produce excessive output

phases.name: Instrumentation Integration (Optional)
phases.objective: Leverage existing instrumentation hooks to automatically track token usage across all supported providers
deliverables: TokenTrackingHook implementation that intercepts chat method calls and extracts token data
Registration of hook in core instrumentation system
key_risks: Instrumentation may add overhead or conflict with other hooks

risks.description: Token usage data may not be available or consistently formatted across all LLM providers, leading to incomplete tracking.
risks.mitigation: Implement provider-specific parsing logic for each supported LLM client and add fallback handling when token counts are missing.
risks.contingency: Log a warning with the query ID and provider name if token usage cannot be extracted, but continue processing without interruption.
risks.verification: Assert that all supported ChatProvider implementations return structured token data or gracefully handle missing fields

risks.description: Integrating with the existing instrumentation framework may introduce performance overhead due to additional hook execution.
risks.mitigation: Make hook registration configurable and ensure minimal processing in on_call_end method by using lightweight data extraction only when enabled.
risks.contingency: Provide a flag to disable token tracking hooks at runtime so performance-sensitive deployments can skip this feature entirely.
risks.verification: Assert that enabling/disabling instrumentation hook does not impact query latency beyond acceptable thresholds

risks.description: The new TokenUsage dataclass may introduce breaking changes if used in APIs expecting raw response types from LLM providers.
risks.mitigation: Ensure backward compatibility by returning wrapped responses that include both token usage and original content, or provide opt-in conversion methods.
risks.contingency: Use versioned interfaces or optional fields to preserve existing behavior while introducing new functionality.
risks.verification: Assert that all ChatProvider implementations maintain compatibility with existing API consumers after token usage addition

risks.description: Some LLM providers like Ollama may not expose token counts via their standard APIs, requiring custom middleware or proxy solutions.
risks.mitigation: Document limitations for unsupported providers and provide clear guidance on implementing token counting through third-party integrations if necessary.
risks.contingency: Implement provider-specific configuration options that allow users to specify how token counts are collected or disabled for non-compliant providers.
risks.verification: Assert that unsupported providers gracefully fall back to logging a message indicating lack of support rather than failing

recommended_contingencies: Implement a configurable toggle for enabling/disabling token tracking hooks at runtime to allow performance-sensitive deployments to disable them without code changes
Add comprehensive unit tests covering all supported providers' response parsing logic, including edge cases where token usage data is missing or malformed
Create detailed documentation outlining which LLM providers support token counting and how to enable it for each one
